text: As mankind multiplied on the earth, daughters were born to them.
The 'sons of God' saw these daughters were beautiful and took wives from among them.
The LORD said his Spirit would not contend with mankind forever, and limited human lifespan to 120 years.
Nephilim were on the earth in those days, offspring of the sons of God and human daughters — heroes of old, men of renown.
The LORD saw how great human wickedness had become, every inclination of the heart only evil continually.
The LORD regretted making mankind, and it grieved him deeply.
He resolved to wipe out mankind, animals, and creatures from the earth, for he regretted making them.
But Noah found favor in the eyes of the LORD.
Noah was a righteous, blameless man among his generation, who walked faithfully with God.
Noah fathered three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
The earth had become corrupt and full of violence in God's sight.
God saw the earth was corrupt, for all people had corrupted their ways.
God told Noah he would destroy all people, since the earth was full of violence because of them.
God told Noah to build an ark of cypress wood with rooms, coated in pitch inside and out.
He gave the exact dimensions: 300 cubits long, 50 wide, 30 high.
He specified a roof, a door in the side, and three decks.
God said he would bring floodwaters to destroy every living creature under heaven.
But he would establish his covenant with Noah, who would enter the ark with his wife, sons, and their wives.
Noah was to bring two of every living creature into the ark to keep them alive.
Two of every kind of bird, animal, and creeping thing would come to him to be kept alive.
Noah was also to store up food for himself and the animals.
Noah did everything just as God commanded him.
